notable_excerpts: Our total renewable energy capacity, primarily solar and wind, stands at 189 megawatts.
Biodiversity management plans are now being implemented additionally at seven of our plants and 11 mines.
We achieved recycling of 111% of bauxite residue excluding Utkal, and 102% of ash this year.
16 of 19 Hindalco sites met zero liquid discharge standards.
Safety is a top priority at Hindalco, and I am pleased to report that there were no fatalities this quarter across our Indian operations.
Our LTIFR in the Financial Year 2025 stood at 0.26, slightly higher than last year due to increased project-related activities.
Hindalco has been recognized as the world's most sustainable aluminum Company for the fifth year in a row.
Our disciplined capital allocation, superior execution and focus on high-value segments will help deliver sustainable growth regardless of near-term market volatility.

chain_of_thought: The environmental disclosures are robust, focusing on renewable energy, biodiversity, and waste management. Social aspects highlight safety and community responsibility, though there's room for improvement in diversity and inclusion. Governance is strong in transparency and risk management but lacks specific details on board independence and anti-corruption measures.
strengths: Strong commitment to renewable energy and biodiversity.
High safety standards with no fatalities reported.
weaknesses: Limited information on diversity and inclusion.
Lack of specific governance details such as board independence.
red_flags: Slight increase in LTIFR due to project-related activities.
